rulings.Case Title: MERCY COMMERCIAL CALIFORNIA et al VS. VILO PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION /Appendix And Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, line 3. PLAINTIFFS MERCY COMMERCIAL CALIFORNIA AND MERCY HOUSINGS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION /Appendix And Evidence is DENIED. Section 10.3 is not unambiguous and is reasonably susceptible to interpretations advanced by both parties. Extrinsic evidence is admissible to interpret the contract. Triable issue of facts exist as to purported undisputed facts 42 and 64. = (501/req)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT ANGELA THOMAS' Motion To Disqualify Counsel Continued to March 11, 2015. rulings.Case Title: ERIC BOICE et al VS. HARCOURT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILIT et al (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, line 5. PLAINTIFFS ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE AND YOWIE STROMBERG's Amended Motion For Class Certification DENIED. The class, as proposed, is not susceptible to class certification. (The Court notes that proposed class as defined in the Points and Authorities in support of the motion is different from the proposed class alleged in the complaint. Additionally, the Notice of Motion does not contain a class definition at all). Plaintiff's fail to show numerocity and common issues, uniform policy or pattern of requiring occupants to check out/reregister for the purpose of maintaining transient status. Plaintiff also fails to show that the proposed class representatives qualify as "an occupant" under CC 1940.1. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN MCDONALD ET AL VS. JASHVANT M. PATEL ET AL

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILIT VS. CHARLES E GASPERI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, line 13 DEFENDANTS CHARLES GASPERI AND BRANDON MORRIS' Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint DENIED. Notice and all supporting documents sufficiently apprise the tenants of their re-occupancy rights. Ellis Act does not require that landlord provide notice to tenants in one unit regarding extension of time for withdrawal of other units. Complaint sufficiently alleges requisite relationship between plaintiff and defendants (1-5). = (501/req)
